title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We want reviews of zoos, wildlife parks, or animal sanctuaries. Have you visited one recently? Write a review describing your experience and saying whether you think such places are beneficial for animals and visitors.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Review: Wildwood Animal Sanctuary

Wildwood Animal Sanctuary, set in a beautiful woodland just outside the city, offers a genuinely different experience from a traditional zoo. Rather than displaying animals for entertainment, it focuses on rehabilitating injured and orphaned British wildlife before releasing them back into the wild.

The sanctuary is home to a wonderful range of native species including foxes, hedgehogs, red squirrels, and several birds of prey. Enclosures are spacious and clearly designed with the animals' wellbeing as the priority. Informative signs explain each animal's story and why it is being cared for, which makes the visit feel educational rather than purely recreational.

Staff members are passionate and knowledgeable, and there are excellent interactive talks for both children and adults. The setting — winding woodland paths with beautiful views — adds enormously to the experience.

At £12 for adults and £8 for children, it represents excellent value, and the entry fee directly funds the rescue work.

This is conservation done right. I left feeling inspired rather than conflicted. Strongly recommended. Rating: 5/5

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You read a news article about plans to ban dogs from a popular local park. Write a letter to the local council expressing your view on this proposal, considering the impact on dog owners and local wildlife, and suggesting a compromise solution.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Members of the Council,

I am writing to share my concerns regarding the proposed ban on dogs in Riverside Park, as reported in last week's local newspaper. While I understand and respect the reasons behind this proposal — particularly the protection of nesting birds in the spring months — I believe a complete ban would be an unnecessarily extreme measure.

Riverside Park is an important outdoor space for hundreds of local families and dog owners who rely on it for daily exercise. Many of these residents, particularly the elderly, depend on their dogs for companionship and motivation to remain active. Removing access to the park entirely would have a significant impact on their quality of life.

I would like to propose a compromise: rather than a complete ban, the council could designate specific dog-free zones during sensitive nesting periods, from March to June. Dogs could remain welcome in other areas of the park, provided they are kept on leads.

I hope the council will consider this balanced approach.

Yours sincerely,
Charlotte Webb

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt:
Your English friend Chris is thinking about getting a pet for the first time and has asked for your advice. Write an email to Chris discussing the responsibilities involved in owning a pet, recommending a suitable pet based on Chris's lifestyle, and sharing your own experience with animals.

Write your email to Chris.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Hi Chris,

How exciting that you're thinking about getting a pet! It's one of the best decisions I've ever made, but you're right to think it through carefully first — it's a big responsibility.

The most important thing to consider is your lifestyle. How much time do you spend at home? Do you have a garden? Can you afford regular vet bills and food costs? These questions will really shape which pet is right for you.

If you're away from home a lot, a dog probably isn't the best choice — they need company and regular walks throughout the day. In that case, a cat might suit you much better, as they are more independent. If you want something lower maintenance, guinea pigs or a small aquarium of fish can be really rewarding.

I've had my cat Felix for three years, and honestly, he has made my flat feel so much more like a home. The daily routine of caring for another creature is genuinely good for your mental health.

Whatever you choose, you won't regret it!

Best wishes,
Sara
